Title: Re: Virus
Notes:
Re: Virus 

User Chuck reported seeing pop-up virus ads on his computer. After investigation, I identified and blocked the malicious source link responsible for triggering the ads. A full system malware scan was performed to verify there were no active threats or infections. The computer is now confirmed safe and operating normally. 

Actions Taken: 

  • Blocked the malicious source link. 

  • Performed a full malware scan and verified system integrity. 

  • Confirmed the issue is resolved and device is secure.

Title: Old laptop that has Administrative locks on it
Notes: Debra, a client, called for assistance with her old laptop's Wi-Fi connectivity. I helped her by guiding her to install MyRemote to resolve the issue. After fixing the Wi-Fi, I prepared the laptop for personal use by removing technician tools and running pending updates. Since Windows updates were restricted due to domain policies, I planned to disconnect the laptop from the domain. A meeting was scheduled for 1 p.m. PST the following day to walk Debra through the process of upgrading her laptop to Windows 11 and transitioning it for personal use.

Title: Old laptop that has Administrative locks on it
Notes: I had a scheduled call with Debra at 1PM PST to guide her through the process of removing her laptop from the domain. I logged into her computer and explained the process, then proceeded to remove the device from the domain. Afterward, I created a local user for her and ran Windows security updates. We reviewed her laptop's specifications and found it didn’t meet the requirements for Windows 11, which I informed her about. I also checked her update policy in the office portal, enrolled it, and confirmed updates will continue until October 2026. Finally, I logged into her email, installed all Office apps (Excel, Word, Outlook), and ensured the computer is now ready for her personal use.

Title: Clean up backup storage in DC.
Notes: Logged into QNAP storage for BKP-CCC and then start cleaning up the space on the qnap. Delete old snapshots and old data from the storage LUN to clear the space on LUN. After that rescan the storage on VBR and then verify the storage location has clear all warning alerts from it.

Title: I am unable to use my Excel Workbook on my work computer today ???
Notes: Debra Platis reported she was unable to use her Excel workbook on her work computer. Logged into her system and found she was experiencing login issues. Disabled her authentication in Microsoft 365 to reset access, then discovered that E3 licenses were unavailable. Assigned her a Microsoft 365 Standard license instead, and then reinstalled 365 apps restoring her Excel access successfully.

Title: Home Office computer
Notes:
Charles reported an issue that he is not able to send the PDF attachment....

so connect to his computer and checked that he has already sent the email...

However he said that he has not received the confirmation email from the user....

so checked the mailbox and informed that the mail has been sent and there was no bounceback email....so mail has been sent..

May be the user has not seen yet....so he said he will call the user and check with him if required he will let us know...

hence closing the ticket.....

Title: SQL Backup Not Syncing to OneDrive
Notes:
gged into the server to investigate why the SQL backup was not moving to OneDrive.

Verified that the backup was successfully completed on the SQL Server, but it was not syncing to OneDrive.

Checked the scheduled task and found an issue with the scheduler. Re-ran the scheduler manually and monitored the process.

After re-running, confirmed that the backup has now successfully moved to OneDrive.

Title: My Remote version 25.9.9.9533 updated on CCC Servers

Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine  . Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 25.9.9.9533. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.

Title: Device Non-Compliant Due to Missing Patche CCC-DC-TSVDI-01,CCC-DC-AD-01,CCC-DC-SQL-02
Notes:
Devices CCC-DC-TSVDI-01, CCC-DC-AD-01, and CCC-DC-SQL-02 were identified as non-compliant due to missing patches.

Logged in to the ManageEngine portal and verified pending updates on all affected systems. Proceeded with patch remediation by installing the following updates:

  • Microsoft Defender Antivirus – Version 1.445.699.0
  • Google Chrome (x64) – Versions 146.0.7680.153 / 146.0.7680.154

After installation, performed a manual patch scan on all systems to verify successful deployment and to clear the non-compliant status.

Post validation, confirmed that all patches have been successfully applied and no high vulnerabilities remain. All systems are now compliant as per patch management policy.
